stimulus: Listening to recorded music in the early 1900s required bulky, fragile equipment, but this changed with the 1948 introduction of the long-playing record, a durable disc that played up to 25 minutes per side. LP records helped to ______blank recorded music: they made it easy and affordable for ordinary households to build music collections, and listeners at the time eagerly shared new albums with friends.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice B is the best answer because "popularize" means to make widely known or enjoyed, and the text says LP records made building a music collection easy and affordable for ordinary households and that listeners eagerly shared albums. Choice A is incorrect because the text describes LPs spreading music, not weakening interest in it. Choice C is incorrect because LPs are described as easier, not more complicated. Choice D is incorrect because the text describes more listening, not less.
